Here's the full picture:

  ★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────

  AgentsView (ghcr.io/kenn-io/agentsview) is a web UI for browsing, searching (full-text), and analyzing Claude Code session transcripts (.jsonl
  files). In rhdh-fullsend it's used specifically to review agent runs from the "fullsend" CI pipeline — both remote (GitHub Actions) and local.

  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

  Wie AgentsView in rhdh-fullsend genutzt wird

  Architektur

  agentsview/
  ├── docker-compose.fullsend.yaml   # Container-Definition
  ├── Makefile                        # Convenience-Targets
  ├── scripts/
  │   ├── fetch-fullsend-runs.sh     # Zieht Transcripts aus GitHub Actions Artifacts
  │   └── import-local-run.sh        # Importiert lokale Agent-Runs
  ├── runs/          (.gitignored)    # Remote-Transcripts
  └── runs-local/    (.gitignored)    # Lokale Transcripts

⏺ Let me explore how agentsview is used in the rhdh-fullsend project.

  Searched for 2 patterns (ctrl+o to expand)

⏺ Found it — there's an agentsview/ directory with its own infrastructure. Let me read the key files.

  Searched for 1 pattern, read 5 files (ctrl+o to expand)

⏺ Now let me check how it's referenced in the broader project context.

  Searched for 1 pattern (ctrl+o to expand)

⏺ Here's the full picture:

  ★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────

  AgentsView (ghcr.io/kenn-io/agentsview) is a web UI for browsing, searching (full-text), and analyzing Claude Code session transcripts (.jsonl files). In rhdh-fullsend it's used
  specifically to review agent runs from the "fullsend" CI pipeline — both remote (GitHub Actions) and local.

  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

  Wie AgentsView in rhdh-fullsend genutzt wird

  Architektur

  agentsview/
  ├── docker-compose.fullsend.yaml   # Container-Definition
  ├── Makefile                        # Convenience-Targets
  ├── scripts/
  │   ├── fetch-fullsend-runs.sh     # Zieht Transcripts aus GitHub Actions Artifacts
  │   └── import-local-run.sh        # Importiert lokale Agent-Runs
  ├── runs/          (.gitignored)    # Remote-Transcripts
  └── runs-local/    (.gitignored)    # Lokale Transcripts

  Was es tut

  1. fetch-fullsend-runs.sh — Holt alle fullsend-* Artifacts (.jsonl Transcripts) von redhat-developer/rhdh-agentic und redhat-developer/rhdh-plugins via gh api. Es organisiert sie als
  runs/<repo>_<agent>/<run-id>_issue-<N>_<transcript>.jsonl und prepended eine Kontext-Zeile (Repo, Issue, Conclusion, URL), damit AgentsView die Sessions sinnvoll anzeigen kann.
  2. import-local-run.sh — Importiert lokale Fullsend-Runs aus $TMPDIR/fullsend (oder einem expliziten Pfad) in runs-local/. Parst das Agent-Directory-Format
  agent-<name>-<issue>-<timestamp>.
  3. Docker Compose — Startet den Container auf Port 8081, mounted die Runs read-only nach /agents/claude (das ist der CLAUDE_PROJECTS_DIR den AgentsView erwartet).

  Make-Targets

  ┌─────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │   Target    │                Was es tut                │
  ├─────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ make fetch  │ Nur Transcripts herunterladen            │
  ├─────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ make up     │ Fetch + Viewer starten                   │
  ├─────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ make local  │ Lokale Runs importieren + Viewer starten │
  ├─────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ make viewer │ Nur Viewer starten (ohne Fetch)          │
  ├─────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ make down   │ Viewer stoppen                           │
  └─────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Integration im Skill

  Der /fullsend runs Subcommand im Fullsend-Skill delegiert an diese Makefile-Targets — so kann man direkt aus Claude Code heraus die Runs browsen lassen.

  Key Detail

  Die Scripts prependen jeder .jsonl-Datei eine synthetische user-Message mit Metadaten (Agent-Name, Repo, Issue-Nummer, Conclusion). Das ist der Trick, damit AgentsView die Sessions
  mit sinnvollen Labels gruppiert anstatt nur Dateinamen zu zeigen.

Ich würde gerne diese Agents View Sache dahingehend erweitern, dass wir auch lokale Sessions da reinbekommen. und zwar ist der Hintergrund, dass wir in einem kleinen Team arbeiten wollen, welches sich die einzelnen agenten sessions des jeweiligen anderen anschauen kann und später auch analysieren kann. Dazu sollen natürlich nicht alle lokalen Sessions geteilt werden, sondern nur die aus einem bestimmten Verzeichnis oder aus einem bestimmten projekt oder vielleicht wo dieser fullcent skill hier installiert ist. Die Sessions sollten entweder in einem Git Repository gespeichert sein. Das ist wahrscheinlich die einfachste Art und Weise zusammenzuarbeiten, sodass man so einen Sessions Repository hat. Dort kann jeder seine Sessions rein pushen und dann kann man sie lokal pullen und dann und Agents View lokal analysieren. Die Frage ist nur, wie man die einzelnen Sessions dann auswählt. Hier könnte man nach Verzeichnis gehen oder was ich eigentlich ganz gut finde, ist das das hier in diesen Full-Send-Skill zu integrieren, so dass man das einmal als Skill-Kommando machen kann, aber auch als Claude-Session-Hook sozusagen. welche claude hooks kommen denn dafür in frage

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- Explore: Fast read-only search agent for locating code. Use it to find files by pattern (eg. "src/components/**/*.tsx"), grep for symbols or keywords (eg. "API endpoints"), or answer "where is X defined / which files reference Y." Do NOT use it for code review, design-doc auditing, cross-file consistency checks, or open-ended analysis — it reads excerpts rather than whole files and will miss content past its read window. When calling, specify search breadth: "quick" for a single targeted lookup, "medium" for moderate exploration, or "very thorough" to search across multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

content.text: Gute Frage — lass mich die verfügbaren Claude Code Hooks durchgehen.

input.prompt:
List ALL available Claude Code hooks (the ones configured in settings.json under "hooks") with their exact trigger names and when they fire. I need the complete list — not just examples, but every hook event type that Claude Code supports. Include:
1. The exact hook key/name
2. When it fires (trigger condition)
3. What context/data is available to the hook script

Focus on hooks that could be relevant for exporting or processing session transcripts — e.g., hooks that fire when a conversation ends, when Claude stops, or at session boundaries.

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have comprehensive documentation. Let me create a complete reference for you.

## Complete Claude Code Hooks Reference

Here is the **complete list** of ALL 29 Claude Code hook event types, when they fire, and what context data is available:

### Session Lifecycle Hooks

| Hook | Trigger | Available Data | Matcher Support |
|:-----|:--------|:---|:---|
| **SessionStart** | Session begins or resumes | `source` (startup/resume/clear/compact), `model`, `agent_type`, `session_title` | `startup`, `resume`, `clear`, `compact` |
| **SessionEnd** | Session terminates | `source` (reason for exit) | `clear`, `resume`, `logout`, `prompt_input_exit`, `bypass_permissions_disabled`, `other` |
| **Setup** | Triggered by `--init-only`, `--init`, or `--maintenance` flags (CI/one-time setup) | `session_id`, `cwd`, hook_event_name | `init`, `maintenance` |

### Turn Hooks (Per User Input)

| Hook | Trigger | Available Data | Matcher Support |
|:-----|:--------|:---|:---|
| **UserPromptSubmit** | After user submits a prompt, before Claude processes | `prompt` text | None (no matcher) |
| **UserPromptExpansion** | When user-typed command expands to full prompt | `command` name | Command names (skill/command triggers) |
| **Stop** | When Claude finishes responding to a turn | `session_id`, `cwd` | None (no matcher) |
| **StopFailure** | Turn ends due to API error | Error details | `rate_limit`, `overloaded`, `authentication_failed`, `oauth_org_not_allowed`, `billing_error`, `invalid_request`, `model_not_found`, `server_error`, `max_output_tokens`, `unknown` |

### Tool Execution Hooks (Per Tool Call)

| Hook | Trigger | Available Data | Matcher Support |
|:-----|:--------|:---|:---|
| **PreToolUse** | Before any tool call executes (can block) | `tool_name`, `tool_input` (args for that tool) | Tool names: `Bash`, `Edit`, `Write`, `Read`, `mcp__*` etc. |
| **PostToolUse** | After a tool call succeeds | `tool_name`, `tool_input`, `tool_output` | Same as PreToolUse |
| **PostToolUseFailure** | After a tool call fails | `tool_name`, `tool_input`, error details | Same as PreToolUse |
| **PostToolBatch** | After a full batch of parallel tool calls completes, before next model call | All tools that ran in batch | None (no matcher) |

### Permission Hooks

| Hook | Trigger | Available Data | Matcher Support |
|:-----|:--------|:---|:---|
| **PermissionRequest** | Permission dialog appears (approval needed) | `tool_name`, `tool_input` | Tool names |
| **PermissionDenied** | Tool call rejected by auto-mode classifier | `tool_name`, `tool_input` | Tool names |

### Notification Hooks

| Hook | Trigger | Available Data | Matcher Support |
|:-----|:--------|:---|:---|
| **Notification** | Claude Code sends a notification | Notification type | `permission_prompt`, `idle_prompt`, `auth_success`, `elicitation_dialog`, `elicitation_complete`, `elicitation_response`, `agent_needs_input`, `agent_completed` |
| **MessageDisplay** | While assistant message text displays (display-only) | Message text | None (no matcher) |

### Subagent & Task Hooks

| Hook | Trigger | Available Data | Matcher Support |
|:-----|:--------|:---|:---|
| **SubagentStart** | Subagent spawned | `agent_type`, `agent_id` | Agent type: `general-purpose`, `Explore`, `Plan`, custom names |
| **SubagentStop** | Subagent finishes | `agent_type`, `agent_id` | Same as SubagentStart |
| **TeammateIdle** | Agent team teammate about to go idle | `agent_id`, agent context | None (no matcher) |
| **TaskCreated** | Task created via TaskCreate | Task details | None (no matcher) |
| **TaskCompleted** | Task marked completed | Task details | None (no matcher) |

### Configuration & File Hooks

| Hook | Trigger | Available Data | Matcher Support |
|:-----|:--------|:---|:---|
| **InstructionsLoaded** | CLAUDE.md or .claude/rules/*.md loaded into context | `source` (reason for load), `file_path` | `session_start`, `nested_traversal`, `path_glob_match`, `include`, `compact` |
| **ConfigChange** | Configuration file changes during session | `source` (file type), `file_path` | `user_settings`, `project_settings`, `local_settings`, `policy_settings`, `skills` |
| **CwdChanged** | Working directory changes (e.g., `cd` command executed) | `cwd` (new directory) | None (no matcher) |
| **FileChanged** | Watched file changes on disk | `file_path`, `change_type` | Literal filenames to watch: `.envrc|.env` |

### Context Compaction Hooks

| Hook | Trigger | Available Data | Matcher Support |
|:-----|:--------|:---|:---|
| **PreCompact** | Before context compaction | Compaction details | `manual`, `auto` |
| **PostCompact** | After context compaction completes | Compaction summary | `manual`, `auto` |

### Worktree Hooks

| Hook | Trigger | Available Data | Matcher Support |
|:-----|:--------|:---|:---|
| **WorktreeCreate** | Worktree being created via `--worktree` or `isolation: "worktree"` | Git/worktree details | None (no matcher) |
| **WorktreeRemove** | Worktree being removed | Worktree path | None (no matcher) |

### MCP Elicitation Hooks

| Hook | Trigger | Available Data | Matcher Support |
|:-----|:--------|:---|:---|
| **Elicitation** | MCP server requests user input during tool call | `mcp_server_name`, form schema | MCP server name: configured server names |
| **ElicitationResult** | User responds to MCP elicitation form | `mcp_server_name`, user response | Same as Elicitation |

---

### Common Input Fields (Available to All Hooks)

Every hook receives these fields via stdin (command hooks) or HTTP POST body:

```json
{
  "session_id": "unique-session-id",
  "prompt_id": "uuid-for-current-prompt",     // v2.1.196+
  "transcript_path": "/path/to/transcript.json",
  "cwd": "/current/working/directory",
  "permission_mode": "default|plan|acceptEdits|auto|dontAsk|bypassPermissions",
  "effort": { "level": "low|medium|high|xhigh|max" },
  "hook_event_name": "SessionStart",           // Name of event that fired
  "agent_id": "subagent-id",                   // When in subagent
  "agent_type": "Explore|security-reviewer"    // When in subagent
}
```

---

### Hooks Relevant for Session Transcript Export

For your use case of **exporting or processing session transcripts**, these are the most relevant:

1. **`SessionEnd`** — Fires when session terminates. Best entry point to capture/export full transcript.
   - Matcher: `clear`, `resume`, `logout`, `prompt_input_exit`, `bypass_permissions_disabled`, `other`
   - Access: `transcript_path` (path to conversation JSON), `session_id`, `cwd`

2. **`Stop`** — Fires after each turn when Claude finishes responding. Can accumulate transcript state per turn.
   - Access: Full event context; can post-process incrementally

3. **`PostCompact`** — Fires after context compaction (when conversation is summarized). May affect transcript representation.
   - Access: Compaction details, matcher: `manual`, `auto`

4. **`InstructionsLoaded`** — Fires when CLAUDE.md or rules loaded. Useful to capture session metadata/instructions.
   - Access: `file_path`, load reason (matcher: `session_start`, `nested_traversal`, etc.)

---

### Hook Configuration in Your settings.json

Your current hooks (from `/Users/mhild/.claude/settings.json`):

```json
{
  "hooks": {
    "Notification": [
      {
        "matcher": "",
        "hooks": [
          {
            "type": "command",
            "command": "/opt/homebrew/Cellar/claude-notify/1.1.0/lib/claude-notify/core/notifier.sh notification"
          }
        ]
      }
    ],
    "Stop": [
      {
        "matcher": "",
        "hooks": [
          {
            "type": "command",
            "command": "/opt/homebrew/Cellar/claude-notify/1.1.0/lib/claude-notify/core/notifier.sh stop"
          }
        ]
      }
    ],
    "PreToolUse": []
  }
}
```

You're currently using:
- **Notification** (all types) → sends notification
- **Stop** (all turns) → sends stop notification
- **PreToolUse** (empty) → no pre-execution hooks configured

---

### References
- Full hooks documentation: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks.md
- Hooks guide with examples: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks-guide.md

content.text:
`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
Claude Code hat **29 Hook-Events**. Für Session-Export sind aber nur wenige relevant — der entscheidende Punkt ist, dass jeder Hook via `stdin` den **`transcript_path`** bekommt, also den Pfad zur aktuellen Session-Datei. Das ist der Schlüssel zum automatischen Exportieren.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

## Relevante Hooks für Session-Export

### 1. **`SessionEnd`** — Der Hauptkandidat

Feuert wenn die Session endet. Matcher: `clear`, `resume`, `logout`, `prompt_input_exit`, `other`.

**Pro:** Einmaliger, sauberer Zeitpunkt — die Session ist komplett, Transcript liegt fertig vor.
**Con:** Feuert nicht bei `Ctrl+C` / Kill — Sessions die abrupt enden werden verpasst.

### 2. **`Stop`** — Nach jedem Turn

Feuert jedes Mal wenn Claude mit einer Antwort fertig ist.

**Pro:** Inkrementell — man könnte nach jedem Turn den aktuellen Stand sichern, also auch abgebrochene Sessions erfassen.
**Con:** Feuert *sehr oft* — das Script müsste idempotent sein und nur bei Bedarf kopieren (z.B. debounced oder nur als "dirty flag").

### 3. **`PostCompact`** — Nach Kontext-Komprimierung

Eher Nische — interessant nur wenn man den komprimierten Stand archivieren will.

## Welcher Hook passt zu eurem Use-Case?

Für "Team teilt sich Sessions über ein Git-Repo" würde ich **`SessionEnd`** empfehlen, mit einem Fallback-Mechanismus:

```
SessionEnd Hook (settings.json)
  → Script prüft: liegt die Session in einem Projekt wo fullsend installiert ist?
    → ja: kopiert Transcript nach sessions-repo/, committed, pushed
    → nein: ignoriert
```

**Aber:** Der Hook muss entscheiden *welche* Sessions exportiert werden. Dafür gibt es im Hook-Input:

| Feld | Wert |
|------|------|
| `cwd` | Arbeitsverzeichnis → kann gegen Projekt-Liste geprüft werden |
| `transcript_path` | Pfad zur `.jsonl` Datei |
| `session_id` | Eindeutige Session-ID |

Das `cwd` ist der natürlichste Filter: "Exportiere nur Sessions aus Projekten, die ein `.claude/skills/fullsend/` Verzeichnis haben."

## Mein Vorschlag

1. **`SessionEnd`-Hook** in der **Projekt-Settings** (`.claude/settings.json` im Repo, nicht global) — dann feuert er *nur* für Sessions in diesem Projekt
2. Das Hook-Script kopiert das Transcript in ein konfigurierbares Git-Repo (Pfad via `.env` oder Config)
3. **Zusätzlich** ein `/fullsend sessions push`-Subcommand für manuellen Export (z.B. wenn man eine laufende Session schon teilen will, ohne sie zu beenden)

Der Vorteil von Projekt-level Settings: man braucht gar keine `cwd`-Filterung — der Hook existiert nur dort wo er gebraucht wird.

Soll ich das so umsetzen, oder willst du am Ansatz noch was drehen? Offene Fragen wären:
- **Git-Repo-Pfad**: Hardcoded, env-var, oder config-file?
- **Auto-push** bei SessionEnd, oder nur lokales Commit (push explizit via Skill)?

Here is the complete report on the agentsview infrastructure.

---

## Files Examined

| File | Path |
|------|------|
| Makefile | `/Users/mhild/src/rhdh/rhdh-fullsend/agentsview/Makefile` |
| docker-compose | `/Users/mhild/src/rhdh/rhdh-fullsend/agentsview/docker-compose.fullsend.yaml` |

| fetch-fullsend-runs.sh | `/Users/mhild/src/rhdh/rhdh-fullsend/agentsview/scripts/fetch-fullsend-runs.sh` |
| .gitignore | `/Users/mhild/src/rhdh/rhdh-fullsend/agentsview/.gitignore` |

---

## 1. Makefile Targets

The Makefile auto-detects `podman compose` vs `docker compose` (line 3). It defines a reusable `start-viewer` macro that brings up the compose stack and prints the URL.

| Target | What it does |
|--------|-------------|
| `fetch` | Runs `fetch-fullsend-runs.sh` to download GH Actions artifacts into `./runs` |
| `up` | Depends on `fetch`, then starts the AgentsView container (remote runs) |
| `local` | Runs `import-local-run.sh` (with optional `DIR=` override), then starts the container with `AGENTSVIEW_RUNS=./runs-local` so only local runs appear |
| `viewer` | Starts the container without fetching anything (for use after manual imports) |
| `down` | `compose down -v` (stops container and removes the named volume) |
| `help` | Prints usage |

Environment variables: `AGENTSVIEW_PORT` (default 8081), `AGENTSVIEW_HOST` (default localhost).

---

## 2. Docker Compose Setup

Image: `ghcr.io/kenn-io/agentsview:latest`

Key configuration:
- Container port 8080 is published to `0.0.0.0:${AGENTSVIEW_PORT:-8081}`.
- `CLAUDE_PROJECTS_DIR` is set to `/agents/claude` inside the container -- this is how AgentsView discovers Claude session transcripts.
- The runs directory (`${AGENTSVIEW_RUNS:-./runs}`) is bind-mounted read-only at `/agents/claude:ro`. This means the `AGENTSVIEW_RUNS` env var controls which directory is served (either `./runs` for remote, or `./runs-local` for local).
- A named volume `agentsview-fullsend-data` is mounted at `/data` for AgentsView's own state.

---

## 3. Local Run Import (`import-local-run.sh`)

**Auto-discovery logic** (lines 24-43): Without arguments, the script checks `$TMPDIR/fullsend` then `/tmp/fullsend` for directories matching `agent-*`. You can also pass an explicit path.

**Directory name parsing** (lines 72-81): Agent run directories follow the pattern `agent-<name>-<issue>-<timestamp>`. The script parses this into three components:
- `AGENT_NAME` -- everything before the issue number
- `ISSUE_NUM` -- second-to-last numeric segment
- `TIMESTAMP` -- trailing numeric segment (unix epoch)

**Transcript discovery** (line 121): It finds all `.jsonl` files under `*/transcripts/*` paths within each agent directory.

**Output layout** (line 84-97): Files are placed in:
```
runs-local/local_<agent>/local_issue-<N>_<iteration>_<transcript>.jsonl
```
The iteration is extracted from the JSONL path (matching `iteration-[0-9]+`).

**Metadata prepend** (lines 103-118): Each output JSONL file gets a synthetic first line -- a JSON object of `type: "user"` containing a message like `[Local fullsend: <agent>] issue #<N>` with the source path and a synthetic `cwd` of `/fullsend/local_<agent>`. This is what gives the session its label in the AgentsView UI.

Idempotency: If the destination file already exists, the transcript is skipped.

---

## 4. Remote Run Fetch (`fetch-fullsend-runs.sh`)

**Default repos** (lines 21-23): `redhat-developer/rhdh-agentic` and `redhat-developer/rhdh-plugins`. You can override by passing repo slugs as arguments.

**Prerequisites**: `gh` (authenticated) and `jq`.

**Artifact discovery** (lines 43-48): Uses `gh api --paginate` to list all non-expired artifacts whose name starts with `fullsend-`. The agent name is derived by stripping that prefix (`agent_name=${art_name#fullsend-}`).

**Skip logic** (lines 60-63): If any file matching `${RUNS_DIR}/${project_dir}/${run_id}_*.jsonl` already exists, that artifact is skipped entirely.

**Download and extraction** (lines 76-78): `gh run download` fetches the artifact into a temp directory. Inside, the script looks for `agent-*` directories to extract the issue number.

**Output layout** (line 96): Files land in:
```
runs/<repo-name>_<agent>/<run-id>_issue-<N>_<transcript>.jsonl
```

**Metadata prepend** (lines 99-114): Same pattern as local import -- a synthetic `type: "user"` JSON line is prepended with content like `[Fullsend: <agent>] <repo>#<issue> (<conclusion>)` plus the GH Actions run URL. The `cwd` is set to `/fullsend/<project_dir>`.

**Transcript discovery** (line 119): Same as local -- finds `*.jsonl` files under `*/transcripts/*`.

---

## 5. Gitignore

File: `/Users/mhild/src/rhdh/rhdh-fullsend/agentsview/.gitignore`

Contents:
```
/runs/
/runs-local/
```

Both the remote (`runs/`) and local (`runs-local/`) transcript directories are git-ignored. The repo root `.gitignore` has no agentsview-specific entries; the ignore rules live entirely in the agentsview subdirectory.

---

## Key Patterns Summary

**Transcript organization**: AgentsView sees projects as directories and sessions as JSONL files within them. Both scripts create a two-level layout:

- **Project directory** = `<repo>_<agent>` (remote) or `local_<agent>` (local)
- **Session file** = `<run-id>_issue-<N>_<transcript>.jsonl` (remote) or `local_issue-<N>_<iteration>_<transcript>.jsonl` (local)

**Labeling**: Both scripts prepend a synthetic "user" message as the first line of each JSONL file. This message carries the agent name, issue number, conclusion status, and source URL/path. AgentsView renders this as the session's opening context. The `cwd` field is synthetic (`/fullsend/...`) and serves as a project identifier.

**Source format**: Both scripts expect fullsend agent output directories named `agent-<name>-<issue>-<timestamp>`, containing JSONL transcript files at a path matching `*/transcripts/*.jsonl`.

**Idempotency**: Both scripts check for existing output files before writing, making them safe to re-run.

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# Import local fullsend agent runs into AgentsView's runs/ folder.
3	#
4	# Usage:
5	#   ./import-local-run.sh                      # auto-discover from $TMPDIR/fullsend
6	#   ./import-local-run.sh <output-dir>         # all runs in a specific output dir
7	#   ./import-local-run.sh <agent-dir>          # single agent run directory
8	#
9	# Without arguments, searches $TMPDIR/fullsend then /tmp/fullsend for agent-* dirs.
10	# Accepts either fullsend's --output-dir (discovers all agent-* subdirs)
11	# or a single agent run directory.
12	#
13	# Prerequisites: jq
14	#
15	# Directory layout produced (matches AgentsView Claude discovery):
16	#   runs-local/local_<agent>/local_issue-<N>_<iteration>_<transcript>.jsonl
17	
18	set -euo pipefail
19	
20	SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"
21	RUNS_DIR="${RUNS_DIR:-${SCRIPT_DIR}/../runs-local}"
22	
23	INPUT_DIR="${1:-}"
24	if [ -z "$INPUT_DIR" ]; then
25	  # Auto-discover: check $TMPDIR/fullsend first (macOS per-user temp), then /tmp/fullsend
26	  for candidate in "${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/fullsend" "/tmp/fullsend"; do
27	    if [ -d "$candidate" ] && compgen -G "${candidate}/agent-*" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
28	      INPUT_DIR="$candidate"
29	      break
30	    fi
31	  done
32	  if [ -z "$INPUT_DIR" ]; then
33	    echo "error: no fullsend output directory found" >&2
34	    echo "" >&2
35	    echo "Searched:" >&2
36	    echo "  ${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/fullsend" >&2
37	    echo "  /tmp/fullsend" >&2
38	    echo "" >&2
39	    echo "Run with an explicit path or set TMPDIR:" >&2
40	    echo "  $0 /path/to/fullsend-output-dir" >&2
41	    exit 1
42	  fi
43	  echo "Auto-discovered: $INPUT_DIR"
44	fi
45	
46	command -v jq >/dev/null 2>&1 || { echo "error: jq is required" >&2; exit 1; }
47	
48	INPUT_DIR="$(cd "$INPUT_DIR" && pwd)"
49	
50	# Collect agent directories: either the input itself or agent-* children
51	AGENT_DIRS=()
52	if [[ "$(basename "$INPUT_DIR")" == agent-* ]]; then
53	  AGENT_DIRS+=("$INPUT_DIR")
54	else
55	  while IFS= read -r -d '' d; do
56	    AGENT_DIRS+=("$d")
57	  done < <(find "$INPUT_DIR" -maxdepth 1 -type d -name 'agent-*' -print0)
58	fi
59	
60	if [ ${#AGENT_DIRS[@]} -eq 0 ]; then
61	  echo "error: no agent-* directories found in $INPUT_DIR" >&2
62	  exit 1
63	fi
64	
65	echo "Found ${#AGENT_DIRS[@]} agent run(s) in $INPUT_DIR"
66	echo
67	
68	total_found=0
69	total_skipped=0
70	
71	for AGENT_DIR in "${AGENT_DIRS[@]}"; do
72	  DIRNAME="$(basename "$AGENT_DIR")"
73	
74	  # Parse: agent-<name>-<issue>-<timestamp>
75	  # Last segment = timestamp (digits), second-to-last = issue (digits), rest = agent name
76	  DIRNAME_NO_PREFIX="${DIRNAME#agent-}"
77	  TIMESTAMP=$(echo "$DIRNAME_NO_PREFIX" | grep -oE '[0-9]+$')
78	  ISSUE_NUM=$(echo "$DIRNAME_NO_PREFIX" | sed "s/-${TIMESTAMP}\$//" | grep -oE '[0-9]+$' || true)
79	  [ -z "$ISSUE_NUM" ] && ISSUE_NUM="unknown"
80	  AGENT_NAME=$(echo "$DIRNAME_NO_PREFIX" | sed "s/-${ISSUE_NUM}-${TIMESTAMP}\$//")
81	
82	  # Derive ISO timestamp from the unix epoch in the directory name
83	  created_at=$(date -u -r "$TIMESTAMP" +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ" 2>/dev/null || date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
84	
85	  DEST_DIR="${RUNS_DIR}/local_${AGENT_NAME}"
86	  mkdir -p "$DEST_DIR"
87	
88	  echo "--- $DIRNAME ---"
89	  echo "  Agent: $AGENT_NAME | Issue: $ISSUE_NUM"
90	
91	  found=0
92	  skipped=0
93	
94	  while IFS= read -r -d '' jsonl; do
95	    iter=$(echo "$jsonl" | grep -oE 'iteration-[0-9]+' | head -1 || echo "iteration-1")
96	    dest_file="${DEST_DIR}/local_issue-${ISSUE_NUM}_${iter}_$(basename "$jsonl")"
97	
98	    if [ -f "$dest_file" ]; then
99	      skipped=$((skipped + 1))
100	      continue
101	    fi
102	
103	    meta_line=$(jq -nc \
104	      --arg agent "$AGENT_NAME" \
105	      --arg issue "$ISSUE_NUM" \
106	      --arg src "$AGENT_DIR" \
107	      --arg ts "$created_at" \
108	      --arg cwd "/fullsend/local_${AGENT_NAME}" \
109	      '{
110	        type: "user",
111	        timestamp: $ts,
112	        message: {
113	          content: ("[Local fullsend: \($agent)] issue #\($issue)\nSource: \($src)")
114	        },
115	        cwd: $cwd
116	      }')
117	
118	    { echo "$meta_line"; cat "$jsonl"; } > "$dest_file"
119	    echo "  -> local_${AGENT_NAME}/$(basename "$dest_file")"
120	    found=$((found + 1))
121	  done < <(find "$AGENT_DIR" -name '*.jsonl' -path '*/transcripts/*' -print0)
122	
123	  if [ "$found" -eq 0 ] && [ "$skipped" -eq 0 ]; then
124	    echo "  (no transcripts)"
125	  fi
126	
127	  total_found=$((total_found + found))
128	  total_skipped=$((total_skipped + skipped))
129	done
130	
131	echo
132	echo "Done: ${total_found} imported, ${total_skipped} already existed"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Import local fullsend agent runs into AgentsView's runs/ folder.
#
# Usage:
#   ./import-local-run.sh                      # auto-discover from $TMPDIR/fullsend
#   ./import-local-run.sh <output-dir>         # all runs in a specific output dir
#   ./import-local-run.sh <agent-dir>          # single agent run directory
#
# Without arguments, searches $TMPDIR/fullsend then /tmp/fullsend for agent-* dirs.
# Accepts either fullsend's --output-dir (discovers all agent-* subdirs)
# or a single agent run directory.
#
# Prerequisites: jq
#
# Directory layout produced (matches AgentsView Claude discovery):
#   runs-local/local_<agent>/local_issue-<N>_<iteration>_<transcript>.jsonl

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"
RUNS_DIR="${RUNS_DIR:-${SCRIPT_DIR}/../runs-local}"

INPUT_DIR="${1:-}"
if [ -z "$INPUT_DIR" ]; then
  # Auto-discover: check $TMPDIR/fullsend first (macOS per-user temp), then /tmp/fullsend
  for candidate in "${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/fullsend" "/tmp/fullsend"; do
    if [ -d "$candidate" ] && compgen -G "${candidate}/agent-*" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      INPUT_DIR="$candidate"
      break
    fi
  done
  if [ -z "$INPUT_DIR" ]; then
    echo "error: no fullsend output directory found" >&2
    echo "" >&2
    echo "Searched:" >&2
    echo "  ${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/fullsend" >&2
    echo "  /tmp/fullsend" >&2
    echo "" >&2
    echo "Run with an explicit path or set TMPDIR:" >&2
    echo "  $0 /path/to/fullsend-output-dir" >&2
    exit 1
  fi
  echo "Auto-discovered: $INPUT_DIR"
fi

command -v jq >/dev/null 2>&1 || { echo "error: jq is required" >&2; exit 1; }

INPUT_DIR="$(cd "$INPUT_DIR" && pwd)"

# Collect agent directories: either the input itself or agent-* children
AGENT_DIRS=()
if [[ "$(basename "$INPUT_DIR")" == agent-* ]]; then
  AGENT_DIRS+=("$INPUT_DIR")
else
  while IFS= read -r -d '' d; do
    AGENT_DIRS+=("$d")
  done < <(find "$INPUT_DIR" -maxdepth 1 -type d -name 'agent-*' -print0)
fi

if [ ${#AGENT_DIRS[@]} -eq 0 ]; then
  echo "error: no agent-* directories found in $INPUT_DIR" >&2
  exit 1
fi

echo "Found ${#AGENT_DIRS[@]} agent run(s) in $INPUT_DIR"
echo

total_found=0
total_skipped=0

for AGENT_DIR in "${AGENT_DIRS[@]}"; do
  DIRNAME="$(basename "$AGENT_DIR")"

  # Parse: agent-<name>-<issue>-<timestamp>
  # Last segment = timestamp (digits), second-to-last = issue (digits), rest = agent name
  DIRNAME_NO_PREFIX="${DIRNAME#agent-}"
  TIMESTAMP=$(echo "$DIRNAME_NO_PREFIX" | grep -oE '[0-9]+$')
  ISSUE_NUM=$(echo "$DIRNAME_NO_PREFIX" | sed "s/-${TIMESTAMP}\$//" | grep -oE '[0-9]+$' || true)
  [ -z "$ISSUE_NUM" ] && ISSUE_NUM="unknown"
  AGENT_NAME=$(echo "$DIRNAME_NO_PREFIX" | sed "s/-${ISSUE_NUM}-${TIMESTAMP}\$//")

  # Derive ISO timestamp from the unix epoch in the directory name
  created_at=$(date -u -r "$TIMESTAMP" +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ" 2>/dev/null || date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")

  DEST_DIR="${RUNS_DIR}/local_${AGENT_NAME}"
  mkdir -p "$DEST_DIR"

  echo "--- $DIRNAME ---"
  echo "  Agent: $AGENT_NAME | Issue: $ISSUE_NUM"

  found=0
  skipped=0

  while IFS= read -r -d '' jsonl; do
    iter=$(echo "$jsonl" | grep -oE 'iteration-[0-9]+' | head -1 || echo "iteration-1")
    dest_file="${DEST_DIR}/local_issue-${ISSUE_NUM}_${iter}_$(basename "$jsonl")"

    if [ -f "$dest_file" ]; then
      skipped=$((skipped + 1))
      continue
    fi

    meta_line=$(jq -nc \
      --arg agent "$AGENT_NAME" \
      --arg issue "$ISSUE_NUM" \
      --arg src "$AGENT_DIR" \
      --arg ts "$created_at" \
      --arg cwd "/fullsend/local_${AGENT_NAME}" \
      '{
        type: "user",
        timestamp: $ts,
        message: {
          content: ("[Local fullsend: \($agent)] issue #\($issue)\nSource: \($src)")
        },
        cwd: $cwd
      }')

    { echo "$meta_line"; cat "$jsonl"; } > "$dest_file"
    echo "  -> local_${AGENT_NAME}/$(basename "$dest_file")"
    found=$((found + 1))
  done < <(find "$AGENT_DIR" -name '*.jsonl' -path '*/transcripts/*' -print0)

  if [ "$found" -eq 0 ] && [ "$skipped" -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "  (no transcripts)"
  fi

  total_found=$((total_found + found))
  total_skipped=$((total_skipped + skipped))
done

echo
echo "Done: ${total_found} imported, ${total_skipped} already existed"

# runs

Browse, search, and analyze fullsend agent run transcripts using AgentsView.

## Overview

Downloads fullsend agent transcripts from GitHub Actions artifacts and serves them in [AgentsView](https://github.com/kenn-io/agentsview) — a web UI for browsing, searching (FTS), and tracking cost across all agent sessions.

Sessions are grouped by repo + agent type (e.g. `rhdh-plugins_review`, `rhdh-agentic_code`). Issue numbers and run metadata are searchable via full-text search.

## Prerequisites

- `gh` CLI authenticated with access to the target repos (for `fetch`/`up`)
- `jq` installed
- Podman or Docker available

## Usage

```
/fullsend runs                    # show status and setup instructions
/fullsend runs fetch              # download all available runs
/fullsend runs up                 # fetch + start AgentsView container
/fullsend runs local [dir]        # import local fullsend runs + start viewer
/fullsend runs viewer             # start viewer without fetching
/fullsend runs down               # stop the container
```

## Procedure

### Status check

If no subcommand is given, check:

1. Does `agentsview/` exist in this repo? If not, tell the user to create it:
   ```bash
   mkdir -p agentsview/scripts
   ```
2. Does `agentsview/runs/` or `agentsview/runs-local/` contain any `.jsonl` files? Report the count and project breakdown for each.
3. Is a container running? Check with:
   ```bash
   podman compose -f agentsview/docker-compose.fullsend.yaml ps 2>/dev/null || \
   docker compose -f agentsview/docker-compose.fullsend.yaml ps 2>/dev/null
   ```
4. Print the URL if running.

### fetch

Run the fetch script:
```bash
cd agentsview && ./scripts/fetch-fullsend-runs.sh
```

The script:
- Paginates through all GitHub Actions artifacts matching `fullsend-*`
- Skips already-downloaded runs (idempotent)
- Extracts transcript JSONLs from artifact archives
- Injects a metadata header with repo, issue, agent, and run URL
- Organizes into `runs/<repo>_<agent>/` directories

Custom repos can be passed as arguments:
```bash
./scripts/fetch-fullsend-runs.sh org/repo1 org/repo2
```

Default repos: `redhat-developer/rhdh-agentic`, `redhat-developer/rhdh-plugins`.

### up

```bash
cd agentsview && make up
```

Or with custom host/port for remote access:
```bash
AGENTSVIEW_HOST=myhost.local AGENTSVIEW_PORT=8082 make up
```

This runs `fetch` first (idempotent), then starts the container.

### local

Import local fullsend runs and start the viewer:

```bash
cd agentsview && make local                                  # auto-discover from $TMPDIR/fullsend
cd agentsview && make local DIR=/tmp/fullsend                # explicit --output-dir
cd agentsview && make local DIR=/tmp/fullsend/agent-triage-3705-1234567890  # single run
```

Without `DIR`, the script auto-discovers runs from `$TMPDIR/fullsend` (macOS per-user temp)
then `/tmp/fullsend`. With `DIR`, accepts either fullsend's `--output-dir` (discovers all
`agent-*` subdirectories) or a single agent run directory. Idempotent — reruns skip
already-imported transcripts.

Local sessions appear in AgentsView under `local_<agent>` project groups
(e.g. `local_my-prs`, `local_triage`), distinguishable from GitHub Actions runs.

### viewer

Start the AgentsView container without fetching or importing — useful when you've already
run `make fetch` or `make local` and just want to restart the viewer:

```bash
cd agentsview && make viewer
```

### down

```bash
cd agentsview && make down
```

## Architecture

```
GitHub Actions artifacts (fullsend-*)       Local fullsend runs (--output-dir)
  │                                           │
  ▼  fetch-fullsend-runs.sh                   ▼  import-local-run.sh
  │                                           │
  ▼                                           ▼
agentsview/runs/                            agentsview/runs-local/
  rhdh-plugins_review/*.jsonl                 local_triage/*.jsonl
  rhdh-agentic_code/*.jsonl                   local_my-prs/*.jsonl
  │                                           │
  │  (make up)                                │  (make local)
  ▼                                           ▼
docker-compose.fullsend.yaml
  AGENTSVIEW_RUNS=./runs (default)    or    AGENTSVIEW_RUNS=./runs-local
  │
  ▼
AgentsView container
  → http://localhost:8081
  → FTS search, analytics, cost tracking
```

Data flow:
- **Remote runs** go to `runs/`, **local runs** go to `runs-local/` — kept separate so `make local` shows only local sessions
- **Index**: SQLite + FTS5 in a Docker volume, cleared on `make down` (`-v`) and rebuilt on next start
- **GitHub artifacts expire after 90 days** — once downloaded, local copies persist

## Searching for runs

In the AgentsView UI:
- Filter by project dropdown to select a repo + agent combination
- Search `#3966` to find all runs for a specific issue
- Search `failure` to find failed runs
- Search tool names like `Bash` or `Read` to find specific tool usage patterns

3	description: |
4	  Fullsend harness validation, drift checking, sandbox debugging, and fullsend setup.
5	  Use when asked to validate fullsend config, check for drift against upstream scaffold,
6	  diff or compare customized harness/env files against upstream, debug a sandbox run,
7	  inspect a fullsend agent run, or look at a specific run by ID or issue number.
8	  Also use when asked why a fullsend run failed, what changed in the harness, or how
9	  to set up env vars for the sandbox.
10	  Also use when asked to trigger a fullsend agent, post a slash command on an issue,
11	  watch or monitor a fullsend run, comment on an issue, or manage labels.
12	  Also use when asked to run sandbox diagnostics, debug the sandbox environment,
13	  check if yarn/corepack/openspec work, or build a custom fullsend agent.
14	  Also use when asked what fullsend agents are, how they work, how to use them,
15	  what agents are available, how to get started with fullsend, or how the local
16	  deployment is configured.
17	  Also use when asked to browse, view, or search fullsend runs in AgentsView,
18	  download run transcripts, start or stop the AgentsView viewer, or check
19	  agent run history.
20	  Also use when asked to upgrade fullsend, sync scaffold files with a new
21	  version, update the CLI, bump fullsend, or check what version we're on.
22	---
23	
24	# /fullsend
25	
26	Tooling for managing fullsend sandbox configurations — validating customized harness/env files against the upstream scaffold, debugging sandbox issues, triggering and monitoring agent runs, and managing issue metadata.
27	
28	<essential_principles>
29	
30	## Essential Principles
31	
32	1. **Customized files are full replacements.** When a repo places a file in `.fullsend/customized/harness/`, fullsend uses it *instead of* the scaffold version — not merged, not overlaid. Any field omitted from the customized file is silently dropped.
33	2. **Upstream scaffold is source of truth.** The canonical harness and env definitions live in the scaffold repo. Customizations must track upstream changes or they drift.
34	3. **Always diff before deploying.** Never commit a customized harness without comparing it field-by-field against the current upstream version.
35	4. **Docker ENV is dead at runtime.** OpenShell strips Containerfile `ENV` directives — they exist during `docker build` but not in the sandbox. All runtime env vars must go through `.env.d/` files.
36	5. **Path flattening.** Fullsend overlays `.fullsend/customized/env/` → `env/`, `.fullsend/customized/harness/` → `harness/`, etc. Harness `host_files.src` paths are always relative to the **flattened** working dir (e.g., `env/foo.env`, never `customized/env/foo.env`).
37	6. **Confirm before mutating.** Commands that write to GitHub (`trigger`, `comment`, `label`) must confirm with the user before acting. These are shared-state actions visible to the whole team.
38	
39	</essential_principles>
40	
41	<repo_resolution>
42	
43	## Repo Resolution
44	
45	All commands that interact with GitHub use the same resolution order:
46	
47	1. **Explicit `--repo owner/name`** flag — highest priority.
48	2. **`FULLSEND_REPO` env var** — if set.
49	3. **cwd detection** — if cwd is inside a git repo with `.fullsend/config.yaml`, derive `owner/name` from `gh repo view --json nameWithOwner -q .nameWithOwner`.
50	4. **Fallback** — check if `../rhdh-agentic` exists and use its `owner/name`.
51	
52	If none resolve, ask the user.
53	
54	</repo_resolution>
55	
56	<env_delivery>
57	
58	## How Env Vars Reach the Sandbox
59	
60	Files in `/sandbox/workspace/.env.d/` are sourced at sandbox startup:
61	```
62	for f in /sandbox/workspace/.env.d/*.env; do [ -f "$f" ] && . "$f"; done
63	```
64	
65	To add a variable, create an env file and wire it via `host_files` in the harness:
66	
67	| What | Where | `expand` | Example |
68	|------|-------|----------|---------|
69	| Runner-side secrets (tokens, credentials) | `env/<name>.env` with `export VAR=${RUNNER_VAR}` | `true` | `GH_TOKEN`, `ISSUE_NUMBER` |
70	| Container toolchain config (fixed paths) | `env/<name>.env` with `export VAR=/fixed/path` | omit (false) | `COREPACK_HOME`, `GOPATH` |
71	| Runner-only vars (never enter sandbox) | `runner_env:` in harness YAML | n/a | `PUSH_TOKEN`, `REPO_DIR` |
72	
73	**`expand: true`** runs `os.ExpandEnv()` on the file using the **GitHub Actions runner's** environment before copying it into the sandbox. `${PATH}` would become the runner's PATH, not the container's. Omit `expand` for hardcoded values.
74	
75	</env_delivery>
76	
77	<setup>
78	
79	## Setup Gates
80	
81	| Gate | Required by | Check | If fail |
82	|------|-------------|-------|---------|
83	| Scaffold dir | `validate` | `$FULLSEND_SCAFFOLD_DIR` or `../asdlc-lab/resources/fullsend-ai/fullsend/internal/scaffold/fullsend-repo/` is a readable directory | Ask user to set `FULLSEND_SCAFFOLD_DIR` or clone `asdlc-lab` |
84	| Target repo | all commands | Repo resolution (see above) | Ask user for the repo |
85	| `gh` CLI | `inspect`, `trigger`, `watch`, `comment`, `label` | `gh auth status` succeeds | Ask user to install and authenticate `gh` |
86	
87	</setup>
88	
89	<intake>
90	
91	## Commands
92	
93	| Command | Description |
94	|---------|-------------|
95	| `validate [repo-path]` | Diff customized harness/env files against upstream scaffold |
96	| `inspect <run-id \| #issue>` | Investigate a fullsend agent run — status, timing, output, logs |
97	| `trigger <agent> <#issue\|#PR> [--repo] [--force]` | Post a fullsend slash command to start an agent |
98	| `watch <#issue\|run-id> [--repo]` | Monitor a triggered run until completion, then auto-inspect |
99	| `debug <#issue> [--repo]` | Run sandbox diagnostics (shortcut for `trigger debug`) |
100	| `comment <#issue> <message> [--repo]` | Post a comment on an issue or PR |
101	| `label <#issue> <add\|remove> <label> [--repo]` | Add or remove a label on an issue or PR |
102	| `runs [fetch\|up\|down]` | Browse fullsend runs in AgentsView — fetch transcripts, start/stop viewer |
103	| `upgrade [version]` | Upgrade CLI, scaffold files, and dispatch workflows to a new fullsend release |
104	| `help [topic]` | Onboarding companion — agent pipeline, local deployment overview, upstream docs |
105	| `custom-agents` | Guide for building custom standalone agents (scaffold, dispatch, security) |
106	
107	If no arguments are given, display this table and ask which the user wants.
108	
109	</intake>
110	
111	<routing>
112	
113	## Routing
114	
115	Parse the first word after `/fullsend` as the subcommand.
116	
117	| Command | Reference |
118	|---------|-----------|
119	| `validate` | `references/validate.md` |
120	| `inspect` | `references/inspect.md` |
121	| `trigger` | `references/trigger.md` |
122	| `watch` | `references/watch.md` |
123	| `debug` | `references/debug.md` |
124	| `comment` | `references/comment.md` |
125	| `label` | `references/label.md` |
126	| `runs` | `references/runs.md` |
127	| `upgrade` | `references/upgrade.md` |
128	| `help` | `references/help.md` |
129	| `custom-agents` | `references/custom-agents.md` |
130	
131	</routing>

<essential_principles>

3. **Always diff before deploying.** Never commit a customized harness without comparing it field-by-field against the current upstream version.
4. **Docker ENV is dead at runtime.** OpenShell strips Containerfile `ENV` directives — they exist during `docker build` but not in the sandbox. All runtime env vars must go through `.env.d/` files.
5. **Path flattening.** Fullsend overlays `.fullsend/customized/env/` → `env/`, `.fullsend/customized/harness/` → `harness/`, etc. Harness `host_files.src` paths are always relative to the **flattened** working dir (e.g., `env/foo.env`, never `customized/env/foo.env`).
6. **Confirm before mutating.** Commands that write to GitHub (`trigger`, `comment`, `label`) must confirm with the user before acting. These are shared-state actions visible to the whole team.

</essential_principles>

<repo_resolution>

## Repo Resolution

All commands that interact with GitHub use the same resolution order:

1. **Explicit `--repo owner/name`** flag — highest priority.
2. **`FULLSEND_REPO` env var** — if set.
3. **cwd detection** — if cwd is inside a git repo with `.fullsend/config.yaml`, derive `owner/name` from `gh repo view --json nameWithOwner -q .nameWithOwner`.
4. **Fallback** — check if `../rhdh-agentic` exists and use its `owner/name`.

If none resolve, ask the user.

</repo_resolution>

<env_delivery>

## How Env Vars Reach the Sandbox

Files in `/sandbox/workspace/.env.d/` are sourced at sandbox startup:
```
for f in /sandbox/workspace/.env.d/*.env; do [ -f "$f" ] && . "$f"; done
```

To add a variable, create an env file and wire it via `host_files` in the harness:

| What | Where | `expand` | Example |
|------|-------|----------|---------|
| Runner-side secrets (tokens, credentials) | `env/<name>.env` with `export VAR=${RUNNER_VAR}` | `true` | `GH_TOKEN`, `ISSUE_NUMBER` |
| Container toolchain config (fixed paths) | `env/<name>.env` with `export VAR=/fixed/path` | omit (false) | `COREPACK_HOME`, `GOPATH` |
| Runner-only vars (never enter sandbox) | `runner_env:` in harness YAML | n/a | `PUSH_TOKEN`, `REPO_DIR` |

**`expand: true`** runs `os.ExpandEnv()` on the file using the **GitHub Actions runner's** environment before copying it into the sandbox. `${PATH}` would become the runner's PATH, not the container's. Omit `expand` for hardcoded values.

</env_delivery>

<setup>

## Setup Gates

| Gate | Required by | Check | If fail |
|------|-------------|-------|---------|
| Scaffold dir | `validate` | `$FULLSEND_SCAFFOLD_DIR` or `../asdlc-lab/resources/fullsend-ai/fullsend/internal/scaffold/fullsend-repo/` is a readable directory | Ask user to set `FULLSEND_SCAFFOLD_DIR` or clone `asdlc-lab` |
| Target repo | all commands | Repo resolution (see above) | Ask user for the repo |
| `gh` CLI | `inspect`, `trigger`, `watch`, `comment`, `label` | `gh auth status` succeeds | Ask user to install and authenticate `gh` |

</setup>

<intake>

## Commands

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `validate [repo-path]` | Diff customized harness/env files against upstream scaffold |
| `inspect <run-id \| #issue>` | Investigate a fullsend agent run — status, timing, output, logs |
| `trigger <agent> <#issue\|#PR> [--repo] [--force]` | Post a fullsend slash command to start an agent |
| `watch <#issue\|run-id> [--repo]` | Monitor a triggered run until completion, then auto-inspect |
| `debug <#issue> [--repo]` | Run sandbox diagnostics (shortcut for `trigger debug`) |
| `comment <#issue> <message> [--repo]` | Post a comment on an issue or PR |
| `label <#issue> <add\|remove> <label> [--repo]` | Add or remove a label on an issue or PR |
| `runs [fetch\|up\|down]` | Browse fullsend runs in AgentsView — fetch transcripts, start/stop viewer |
| `upgrade [version]` | Upgrade CLI, scaffold files, and dispatch workflows to a new fullsend release |
| `help [topic]` | Onboarding companion — agent pipeline, local deployment overview, upstream docs |
| `custom-agents` | Guide for building custom standalone agents (scaffold, dispatch, security) |

If no arguments are given, display this table and ask which the user wants.

</intake>

<routing>

## Routing

Parse the first word after `/fullsend` as the subcommand.

| Command | Reference |
|---------|-----------|
| `validate` | `references/validate.md` |
| `inspect` | `references/inspect.md` |
| `trigger` | `references/trigger.md` |
| `watch` | `references/watch.md` |
| `debug` | `references/debug.md` |
| `comment` | `references/comment.md` |
| `label` | `references/label.md` |
| `runs` | `references/runs.md` |
| `upgrade` | `references/upgrade.md` |
| `help` | `references/help.md` |

</routing>

# Plan: Team Session Sharing via Git Repo + SessionEnd Hook

## Context

The team wants to share Claude Code session transcripts for review and analysis in AgentsView. Currently, only fullsend CI runs (remote) and local fullsend runs can be viewed. The goal is to enable sharing of **regular local Claude Code sessions** from projects where the fullsend skill is installed — committed to a shared git repo, viewable in AgentsView alongside existing runs.

Design decisions (from user):
- Repo path via **configuration** (not hardcoded)
- `SessionEnd` hook does **local commit only** (no auto-push)
- Push available via **skill subcommand**

## Deliverables

### 1. Session export script: `agentsview/scripts/export-session.sh`

Receives session metadata via stdin (JSON from Claude Code hook system) and:

1. Reads `transcript_path`, `session_id`, `cwd` from stdin JSON
2. Reads the sessions repo path from config (see below)
3. Derives a meaningful label from `cwd` (project basename) and `session_id`
4. Copies the transcript into the sessions repo with the established layout:
   ```
   <sessions-repo>/<username>_<project>/<session-id>.jsonl
   ```
5. Prepends the standard AgentsView metadata line (synthetic user message with project, user, timestamp)
6. `git add` + `git commit` in the sessions repo (**no push**)

Username derived from `git config user.name` or `$USER` — so sessions are attributable to team members.

Skip conditions:
- Sessions repo not configured or path doesn't exist → silent exit (not every project needs this)
- Transcript file doesn't exist or is empty → skip
- Session already exported (idempotent via checking if file exists)

### 2. Project-level hook config: `.claude/settings.json`

```json
{
  "hooks": {
    "SessionEnd": [
      {
        "matcher": "",
        "hooks": [
          {
            "type": "command",
            "command": "bash agentsview/scripts/export-session.sh"
          }
        ]
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

By placing this in the **project** settings (not user global), the hook only fires for sessions in this repo. The script itself handles the "is a sessions repo configured?" check gracefully.

### 3. Configuration: `~/.config/fullsend/sessions.env`

Following the existing fullsend config pattern (`~/.config/fullsend/*.env`):

```bash
# Path to the shared git repo for session transcripts
FULLSEND_SESSIONS_REPO=/path/to/team-sessions-repo
```

The export script sources this file. If it doesn't exist or the var is empty, the script exits silently.

### 4. Skill subcommand: `/fullsend sessions`

New reference file `references/sessions.md` with sub-operations:

| Sub-operation | What it does |
|---|---|
| `/fullsend sessions` | Status: is sessions repo configured? How many sessions? Last sync? |
| `/fullsend sessions push` | `git push` in the sessions repo |
| `/fullsend sessions pull` | `git pull` in the sessions repo (get team's sessions) |
| `/fullsend sessions view` | Start AgentsView with `AGENTSVIEW_RUNS=<sessions-repo>` |

### 5. Makefile target: `make sessions`

New target in `agentsview/Makefile`:

```makefile
sessions:
	@SESSIONS_REPO=$$(. ~/.config/fullsend/sessions.env 2>/dev/null && echo $$FULLSEND_SESSIONS_REPO); \
	if [ -z "$$SESSIONS_REPO" ]; then echo "error: FULLSEND_SESSIONS_REPO not set in ~/.config/fullsend/sessions.env" >&2; exit 1; fi; \
	AGENTSVIEW_RUNS=$$SESSIONS_REPO $(start-viewer)
```

## Files to create/modify

| File | Action |
|---|---|
| `agentsview/scripts/export-session.sh` | **Create** — session export script |
| `.claude/settings.json` | **Create** — project-level SessionEnd hook |
| `.claude/skills/fullsend/references/sessions.md` | **Create** — skill reference for `/fullsend sessions` |
| `.claude/skills/fullsend/SKILL.md` | **Modify** — add `sessions` to command table and routing table |
| `agentsview/Makefile` | **Modify** — add `sessions` target |

## Verification

1. **Hook wiring**: Create a `.claude/settings.json`, start a new Claude session in this project, end it, verify the hook ran (check for the commit in the sessions repo)
2. **Script standalone**: Run `echo '{"transcript_path":"/tmp/test.jsonl","session_id":"test-123","cwd":"/Users/mhild/src/rhdh/rhdh-fullsend"}' | bash agentsview/scripts/export-session.sh` with a test JSONL and verify it lands correctly
3. **Skill subcommand**: Run `/fullsend sessions` and verify status output
4. **AgentsView display**: Run `make sessions` and verify shared sessions appear correctly in the viewer
